# Tables and Figures

<!-- Layout mirrors studies/resource_extraction/narrative/sections/98_tables_and_figures.Rmd.
     Tables are built by scripts/305_tables.R; figures by scripts/100_exhibits.R into output/figure/.
     Exhibit titles are markdown headings (not flextable captions) so they appear in the Word navigation pane.
     Landscape exhibits are grouped into ONE section: emitting sec_landscape() then sec_portrait() back to back
     creates an empty portrait section, which Word renders as a blank page.
     A section's properties apply to the content BEFORE it: sec_portrait() closes a portrait run,
     sec_landscape() closes a landscape run. -->

```{r, echo=FALSE, results='asis'}
sec_portrait()
```

#### Table 1. Summary statistics of crop producers in Ghana (1991-2017)

```{r tab1, echo=FALSE}
ft_table1()
```

\newpage

#### Table 2. Farmland ownership details amongst Ghanaian crop farmers (2012-2017)

```{r tab2, echo=FALSE}
ft_table2()
```

\newpage

#### Table 3. Land ownership impact on input elasticities and variability in crop production in Ghana (1991-2017)

```{r tab3, echo=FALSE}
ft_table3()
```

```{r, echo=FALSE, results='asis'}
sec_landscape()
```

#### Table 4. Parity in technology level and technical efficiency by farmland ownership details in Ghana (1991-2017)

```{r tab4, echo=FALSE}
ft_table4()
```

\newpage

#### Figure 1. Crop production input and output gaps associated with farmland ownership in Ghana (1991-2017)

![](../output/figure/input_TE.png){width=6.3in}

\newpage

#### Figure 2. The association between farmland ownership and farm productivity from 1991-2017

![](../output/figure/score_trend.png){width=6.3in}

```{r, echo=FALSE, results='asis'}
sec_portrait()
```

#### Figure 3. Farmland ownership parity in crop production technology adoption and technical efficiency by farmer gender, age, and education in Ghana (1991-2017)

![](../output/figure/heterogeneity_genderAge.png){width=9.3in}

\newpage

#### Figure 4. Farmland ownership parity in crop production technology adoption and technical efficiency by major crops and administrative regions in Ghana (1991-2017)

![](../output/figure/heterogeneity_crop_region.png){width=9.3in}

```{r, echo=FALSE, results='asis'}
sec_landscape()
```
